title: Add trust-flush CronJob sub-reconciler
summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of the trust-flush CronJob sub-reconciler that follows established optional sub-reconciler patterns (HPA, NetworkPolicy). All acceptance criteria are met. CRD types, webhook validation, reconciler wiring, deepcopy, CRD YAML, and documentation are complete and consistent. Two major findings: (1) SecurityContext test uses inline assertions instead of the shared expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper, checking only 2 of 4 PSS Restricted fields; (2) TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue does not include TrustFlushReady in its assertion loop despite the condition being added to subConditionTypes.

tests_checklist.item: Unit tests exist and pass (18 tests)
Integration tests exist with envtest (3 tests)
Edge cases covered (nil spec, empty args, suspend toggle)
Error cases covered (create error, delete error propagation)
Webhook validation tests (valid cron, invalid cron, empty, nil)
Full reconcile condition assertion includes TrustFlushReady

code_quality_checklist.item: CronJob name derived from CR name (multi-instance safe)

security_checklist.item: All volume mounts are ReadOnly: true

architecture_checklist.item: TrustFlushSpec is optional pointer field (*TrustFlushSpec) on KeystoneSpec
TrustFlushReady in subConditionTypes for aggregate Ready
Reconcile call ordered after reconcileBootstrap, before setReadyCondition
No webhook Default() changes (schedule default from CRD marker)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: deleteCronJob is minimal (same pattern as deleteNetworkPolicy)
No unused code or dead paths

fail_fast_checklist.item: Webhook rejects invalid cron before reconciler runs

defensive_checklist.item: Nil guard on spec.TrustFlush before accessing fields
Empty schedule guard in webhook validate()

issues_found.description: TestTrustFlushCronJob_SecurityContext uses inline assertions checking only 2 of 4 SecurityContext fields (AllowPrivilegeEscalation, RunAsNonRoot) instead of the shared expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper from security_context_test.go. Every other reconciler test file (reconcile_fernet_test.go, reconcile_bootstrap_test.go, reconcile_deployment_test.go, reconcile_credential_test.go, reconcile_database_test.go) uses this helper, which also validates ReadOnlyRootFilesystem and SeccompProfile. This violates the mandatory 'Cross-cutting security helpers in dedicated files with shared test assertions' pattern.
issues_found.fix: Replace the inline assertions with a call to expectRestrictedSecurityContext(g, &cronJob.Spec.JobTemplate.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[0]). This ensures all 4 PSS Restricted fields are verified and follows the established pattern.

issues_found.description: TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue exercises a full Reconcile() call and asserts all sub-conditions are True, but the assertion loop at line 294 lists only the original 8 condition types and does not include TrustFlushReady. The production code at keystone_controller.go:43 added TrustFlushReady to subConditionTypes (now 9 entries). A regression that removes the reconcileTrustFlush call from the Reconcile chain would not be caught by this test. This also matches the external review pattern 'Ensure test names accurately describe their coverage scope'.
issues_found.fix:
Add "TrustFlushReady" to the condition type slice in the assertion loop:
for _, condType := range []string{"SecretsReady", "FernetKeysReady", "CredentialKeysReady", "DatabaseReady", "DeploymentReady", "HPAReady", "NetworkPolicyReady", "BootstrapReady", "TrustFlushReady"} {

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a Chainsaw E2E test (tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/) for the trust-flush CronJob lifecycle in a future ticket, matching the E2E coverage of fernet-rotation and network-policy features.
next_steps: Replace inline SecurityContext assertions in TestTrustFlushCronJob_SecurityContext with the shared expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper
Add TrustFlushReady to the assertion loop in TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue
